notes: Use the OpenAPI contract for executable API tool generation.
Use this manifest to rank canonical documentation pages.
Use the sitemap for discovery only.
Prefer raw markdown from the repository when available.

documents.title: Books API Reference
documents.summary: Endpoint reference for the live Books API, including pagination, search, create, patch, delete, and error behavior.

documents.title: Cloud IAM / Workload Identity
documents.summary: Pattern 2 — your app authenticates to cloud services using a **platform-assigned identity**, with no long-lived credentials stored anywhere.

documents.title: Auth Overview — The Big Picture
documents.summary: Decision guide for JWT bearer auth, managed identity, and Microsoft Entra ID in a FastAPI application.

documents.title: Deployment Overview
documents.summary: Multi-cloud deployment overview for the Python Demo API across AWS, GCP, and Azure hosting options.

documents.title: Getting Started
documents.summary: Quick local setup for the Python Demo API, including dependency install, running the FastAPI server, and verifying the Books API.

documents.title: Introduction
documents.summary: Overview of the Python Demo API, its live FastAPI surface, and the docs areas that expand on auth and cloud deployment patterns.

documents.title: Microsoft Entra ID
documents.summary: Pattern 3 — enterprise SSO with MFA, conditional access, and app roles managed in the Azure portal.

documents.title: JWT Bearer Authentication
documents.summary: Pattern 1 — stateless, self-contained tokens for user-facing APIs.

documents.title: Azure App Service
documents.summary: The simplest path to a production FastAPI app on Azure — git push or ZIP deploy, TLS included, Managed Identity with one toggle.

documents.title: AWS Deployment
documents.summary: Deploy FastAPI to AWS with zero long-lived credentials using IAM Roles for Service Accounts (IRSA) or Lambda execution roles.

documents.title: Azure Functions
documents.summary: Deploy FastAPI as a serverless Azure Function using the **Python v2 programming model** — a single-file, decorator-based approach that requires no `function.json` files.

documents.title: GCP Deployment
documents.summary: Deploy FastAPI to Google Cloud with zero long-lived credentials using Workload Identity Federation and Application Default Credentials (ADC).

documents.title: AKS (Azure Kubernetes Service)
documents.summary: Deploy FastAPI on Kubernetes with Workload Identity for pod-level Managed Identity — the modern, secure approach.

documents.title: ECS Fargate
documents.summary: Deploy FastAPI on AWS Fargate — managed containers without EC2 instances to patch. Each task gets its own IAM role via ECS Task IAM Roles.

documents.title: EKS (Elastic Kubernetes Service)
documents.summary: Deploy FastAPI on Amazon EKS using **IAM Roles for Service Accounts (IRSA)** — each pod gets its own IAM role with no node-level credentials.

documents.title: AWS Lambda
documents.summary: Deploy FastAPI as a serverless Lambda function using [Mangum](https://github.com/jordaneremieff/mangum) and [AWS Lambda Powertools](https://docs.powertools.aws.dev/lambda/python/latest/) — the AWS-built toolkit for production observability (Logger, Tracer, Metrics, and Parameters).

documents.title: App Engine
documents.summary: App Engine Standard uses the default App Engine service account bound to your app. Useful for simple APIs that don't need custom networking.

documents.title: Cloud Run
documents.summary: Cloud Run is the simplest GCP deployment: push a container, get a URL. Each revision runs with a bound Google Cloud service account — no key files needed.

documents.title: GKE (Google Kubernetes Engine)
documents.summary: GKE Workload Identity maps a Kubernetes service account to a Google Cloud service account, giving each pod its own IAM identity — no node-level credentials.
